hypothesis: Training E2EFOLD with an auxiliary multi-sample structure-consistency loss that minimizes the average backbone RMSD between the native backbone and the decoder-predicted backbones for k=3 distinct discrete sequences sampled from the encoder per input will increase designed-sequence self-consistency (mean TM-score) by at least 0.05 on RFData and simultaneously increase per-position sequence entropy relative to the original training without this loss.
